rules.position: Confidential Information should cover all non-public information disclosed by either party, whether or not marked, and should include oral, visual, written, electronic, and other disclosed information.
rules.fallback: Confidential Information means all non-public information disclosed by or on behalf of a party, whether before or after the effective date, in any form or medium and whether or not marked confidential.
rules.escalation_trigger: Escalate only if the counterparty refuses coverage for unmarked non-public information.

rules.position: The NDA should apply mutually to both parties and their affiliates when affiliates disclose or receive information.
rules.fallback: Each party and its controlled affiliates may disclose or receive Confidential Information under this Agreement, and each party is responsible for its affiliates' compliance.
rules.escalation_trigger: Escalate affiliate coverage gaps if the transaction requires affiliate participation.

rules.position: Exclusions should be limited to information already known without restriction, public through no breach, independently developed, or received from a lawful third party without duty.
rules.fallback: The exclusions apply only to information the recipient can document was lawfully known without restriction, publicly available through no breach, independently developed without use of Confidential Information, or received from a third party without breach of duty.
rules.escalation_trigger: Escalate if exclusions allow use of information obtained through breach or unsupported recollection.

rules.position: Use should be limited to evaluating and negotiating the contemplated transaction.
rules.fallback: The recipient may use Confidential Information solely to evaluate, negotiate, and, if agreed, perform the contemplated transaction between the parties.
rules.escalation_trigger: Escalate any use right for unrelated internal business purposes or competitive analysis.

rules.position: The care standard must be at least reasonable care and no less than the recipient uses for its own similar confidential information.
rules.fallback: Recipient will protect Confidential Information using at least reasonable care and no less than the care it uses to protect its own similar confidential information.
rules.escalation_trigger: Escalate if the standard falls below reasonable care.

rules.position: Disclosures to representatives should be need-to-know only, and representatives must be bound by confidentiality duties at least as protective as the NDA.
rules.fallback: Recipient may disclose Confidential Information to Representatives only on a need-to-know basis, provided those Representatives are bound by written or professional duties at least as protective as this Agreement.
rules.escalation_trigger: Escalate if representative disclosures are allowed without protective duties.

rules.position: Compelled disclosure should require prompt notice where legally permitted and reasonable cooperation to seek protective treatment.
rules.fallback: Before any compelled disclosure, Recipient will, to the extent legally permitted, provide prompt notice and reasonable cooperation so Discloser may seek protective treatment.
rules.escalation_trigger: Escalate any carve-out from notice or cooperation obligations.

rules.position: Return or destruction should occur promptly upon request, with archival and legal-hold copies remaining protected.
rules.fallback: Upon request, Recipient will promptly return or destroy Confidential Information, except archival or legal-hold copies retained under continuing confidentiality obligations.
rules.escalation_trigger: Escalate if the return/destruction period exceeds 30 days or retained copies are unprotected.

rules.position: Confidentiality obligations should last at least three years, and trade secrets should remain protected as long as they remain trade secrets.
rules.fallback: Confidentiality obligations continue for three years after disclosure, and trade secrets remain protected for so long as they qualify as trade secrets under applicable law.
rules.escalation_trigger: Escalate if survival is shorter than three years or trade secrets expire automatically.

rules.position: The NDA should not grant licenses or ownership rights in Confidential Information.
rules.fallback: No license, assignment, or ownership interest is granted by disclosure of Confidential Information except the limited right to use it as expressly permitted by this Agreement.
rules.escalation_trigger: Escalate any express IP license beyond evaluation use.

rules.position: The agreement should preserve equitable relief for unauthorized use or disclosure.
rules.fallback: Each party acknowledges that unauthorized use or disclosure may cause irreparable harm for which equitable relief may be appropriate, without limiting other remedies.
rules.escalation_trigger: Escalate if equitable relief is waived.

rules.position: Residual knowledge clauses should be absent unless expressly approved by the client.
rules.fallback: Delete the residual knowledge clause unless expressly approved by the client in writing.

rules.position: A standard NDA does not require a non-solicit covenant; the absence of one is acceptable and should not be flagged.
rules.fallback: No change; absence of a non-solicit covenant is acceptable.
rules.escalation_trigger: Never escalate solely because a non-solicit covenant is absent.

rules.position: New York governing law is acceptable for this engagement and should not be flagged as an issue.
rules.fallback: No change; New York governing law is acceptable for this smoke-test NDA.
rules.escalation_trigger: Never escalate solely because the agreement uses New York law.
